title: HUD CDBG Action Plan, Senior Tax Exemption Expansion, and Federal Compliance Concerns
summary: Three major topics. (1) Michelle Spansky presents items 37-38: HUD Year 52 annual action plan for CDBG, ESG, and HOME entitlement grants; public meeting drew ~15 attendees with 10 speakers. (2) Assessment Commissioner presents senior citizen property tax exemption expansion under NYS RPTL Section 467: extends eligibility to those earning up to $46,999.99 with up to 65% partial exemption. Staff processed 2,800 applications last year. Councilor Caldwell has championed this for months. (3) Councilor Ehrenreich raises concerns about Trump administration requiring immigration documentation from CDBG-funded agencies. Deputy Commissioner Spansky and Commissioner Michael Collins (NBD) confirm current court injunctions prevent enforcement; agencies are NOT being asked to collect that information. Executive session with corporation counsel recommended for detailed legal discussion.

political_signal.description: Councilor Caldwell champions senior exemption expansion she has pushed for months; council also vigilant about protecting CDBG-funded agencies from premature federal compliance

processing_notes: Department of Permits and Development detection from development keyword; actual presenting dept is Neighborhood and Business Development
Trump admin CDBG documentation requirements are subject to active litigation with NYS as party; injunctions currently in effect
$300 detected amount refers to constituent being $300 over the senior exemption income limit
NBD = Department of Neighborhood and Business Development
Segment 16 of 19 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday February 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: national grid -> National Grid
